title: acacia coriacea subsp. coriacea
intro: Acacia coriacea subsp. coriacea, also known as the broad-leaved wattle or rusty wattle, is a species of wattle that is native to Australia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Acacia coriacea subsp. coriacea include its ability to treat wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues, as well as its use as an anti-diarrheal agent. Acacia coriacea subsp. coriacea is often prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and its bark, leaves, and flowers are used in traditional medicine.
